data.title: Polymarket's prediction that "the next US-Iran diplomatic meeting will be held in Pakistan" briefly rose to 59%, a 21% increase in 24 hours.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in Polymarket's prediction event of "Where will the next US-Iran diplomatic meeting be held?", the probability of Pakistan's participation surged to 59% this morning, a 21% increase in the past 24 hours. Earlier today, news broke that Trump stated he had begun indirect negotiations with Iran through a Pakistani "envoy," and that progress was going well. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before they are priced in.

data.title: AI Concerns Prompt Private-Credit Funds to Reassess Software Investments
data.description:
Private-credit fund managers are increasingly downplaying their investments in software amid growing concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on the industry. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this shift comes as AI technologies continue to evolve, raising questions about the future viability of certain software investments.

The analysis highlights that fund managers are becoming cautious, reassessing their portfolios to mitigate potential risks associated with AI advancements. This trend reflects broader apprehensions in the financial sector about how AI could disrupt traditional software markets.

As AI continues to develop, its potential to replace or significantly alter existing software solutions is prompting fund managers to reconsider their strategies. The move underscores the need for adaptability in investment approaches as technological innovations reshape industries.

The report suggests that while AI presents opportunities for growth, it also poses challenges that require careful navigation by investors. Fund managers are thus balancing the promise of AI-driven advancements with the uncertainties they bring to the software sector.

data.title: Hong Kong ADRs and Night Futures Decline Amid U.S. Stock Market Drop
data.description: Hong Kong's ADRs experienced a decline of 11 points, or 0.05%, closing at 24,940 points following a downturn in the U.S. stock market. According to Ming Pao, the night futures also saw a decrease, falling by 221 points, or 0.89%, to settle at 24,630 points, with a discount of 322 points.

data.title: Iran's Role in Determining War Duration Highlighted by Tehran
data.description: A senior Iranian security official has indicated that Tehran will decide when the ongoing conflict will end, suggesting Iran is prepared to maintain its offensive for an extended period. According to Ming Pao, this statement counters the U.S. assessment that the conflict might conclude within weeks. The remarks underscore Iran's strategic position and its readiness to prolong the engagement, challenging U.S. perspectives on the timeline of the war.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Remains Below Threshold
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index is currently at 48, which is below the 75-point threshold required to declare an altcoin season. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the 90-day price performance of the top 100 coins by market capitalization, excluding stablecoins and wrapped coins, in comparison to Bitcoin.

data.title: Account with over $2.2 million in profits and a total purchase of $100,000. (NBA regular season game: Thunder defeat Knicks)
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, an account with over $2.2 million in profits (address: 0xee00ba338c59557141789b127927a55f5cc5cea1) purchased $100,000 worth of bets on the Thunder defeating the Knicks in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Thunder vs. Knicks" prediction event. The opening price was 77¢, and the current price is 72¢. The NBA regular season game between the Thunder and the Knicks started at 7:30 AM Beijing time today. We are currently in halftime, and the score is temporarily 53-52, with the Thunder leading by one point. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are set.

data.title: Australian Fuel Prices Hit Record High Amid Iran Conflict Impact
data.description: Australian fuel prices reached an unprecedented level last week due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the situation's impact on the economy. The surge in prices has led policymakers to contemplate reducing certain road taxes and implementing additional measures to alleviate the financial burden on households and businesses. The government is actively exploring strategies to mitigate the economic strain caused by the escalating fuel costs.

data.title: US media: Hundreds of US special operations personnel have arrived in the Middle East.
data.description: According to The New York Times, two U.S. military officials said on Sunday that hundreds of U.S. special operations forces personnel have arrived in the Middle East to join thousands of Marines and Army paratroopers in the deployment. This move is intended to provide President Trump with more tools to escalate the month-long military conflict with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Predicts Imminent Collapse of Cuba
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that Cuba is on the brink of collapse and that the United States will soon provide assistance. According to BlockBeats, Trump made these remarks on March 30, emphasizing the urgency of the situation. The President's comments highlight ongoing tensions and the potential for significant geopolitical shifts in the region.

data.title: U.S. Military Deploys Special Forces to Middle East Amid Tensions with Iran
data.description: According to Odaily, two U.S. military officials stated on Sunday that hundreds of American special operations forces have arrived in the Middle East. They join thousands of Marines and Army paratroopers in this deployment. This move aims to provide U.S. President Donald Trump with additional options to escalate the month-long military conflict with Iran.

data.title: Trump Reports Progress in Indirect Talks with Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that indirect negotiations with Iran are advancing positively. According to NS3.AI, Trump revealed that 20 Pakistani-flagged oil tankers have been permitted passage through the Strait of Hormuz. However, he did not provide specifics on the possibility of a ceasefire or the reopening of the strait in the near future. Trump expressed optimism, stating that an agreement is anticipated soon.

data.title: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda: Exchange Rate Fluctuations May Affect Core Inflation
data.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda has stated that fluctuations in exchange rates could influence core inflation through changes in inflation expectations. According to Jin10, Ueda emphasized the potential impact of currency movements on the broader economic landscape, highlighting the interconnectedness of exchange rates and inflation dynamics. His remarks underscore the importance of monitoring exchange rate trends as they may have significant implications for Japan's economic policy and inflation targets.

data.title: Japanese Stocks Fall Amid Iran Conflict and Rising Oil Prices
data.description: Japanese stocks experienced a decline as concerns over the escalating conflict in Iran and rising oil prices affected investor risk appetite. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing conflict, now in its fifth week, on market sentiment. The situation has led to increased caution among investors, contributing to the downturn in the stock market. The conflict's progression and its influence on oil prices continue to be closely monitored by market participants.

data.title: Trump: Cuba will collapse in a short time, and the United States will provide aid.
data.description: Trump stated that Cuba will collapse in a short time, and the United States will provide aid. (Jinshi)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Middle Eastern Aluminum Producers Attacked, LME Prices Surge
data.description: Two Middle Eastern aluminum producers were attacked by Iran on Saturday, leading to a significant impact on global markets. According to Ming Pao, the London Metal Exchange (LME) saw aluminum prices rise by 6% during early Asian trading, reaching $3,492 per ton. This incident highlights the challenges that conflicts pose to critical industries and the global economy.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $67,000 Amid Daily Gains
data.description: Bitcoin has recently exceeded the $67,000 mark, according to PANews. The cryptocurrency is currently priced at $67,044.50, reflecting a daily increase of 0.78%.

data.title: Nikkei 225 Index Experiences Significant Drop
data.description: The Nikkei 225 Index saw a sharp decline of 5% during the day, currently standing at 50,627.52 points. According to ChainCatcher, this drop reflects ongoing volatility in the market. The index's performance is closely watched as it is a key indicator of economic trends in Japan.

data.title: Walmart's OnePay adds more than ten new crypto tokens
data.description: Walmart-owned OnePay recently added several tokens, including SUI, Polygon, and Arbitrum, to its crypto service. Previously, it had already listed assets such as Solana, Cardano, Bitcoin Cash, and PAX Gold, bringing the total number of newly added tokens to over ten. OnePay launched its crypto trading service in January of this year, initially supporting only Bitcoin and Ethereum. Its spokesperson stated that the platform prioritizes user demand, liquidity, and the regulatory environment when expanding its asset offerings, emphasizing long-term practicality rather than chasing trends. OnePay also provides financial services such as savings, credit cards, and loans, and its digital wallet can be used for Walmart's offline and online payment scenarios. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Trump: Indirect negotiations with Iran are progressing well, and an agreement is expected soon.
data.description: According to the Financial Times, despite US President Trump's threats to seize control of Iran's oil production, he also emphasized that indirect negotiations with Iran through a Pakistani "envoy" were progressing well. When asked if a ceasefire agreement and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz were possible in the coming days, Trump declined to provide specific details. He stated, "We still have about 3,000 targets left to bomb—we've bombed 13,000 so far—and several thousand more to go. An agreement is expected soon." Last week, Trump claimed that Iran had allowed 10 Pakistani-flagged oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, a move he described as "a gift to the White House." He stated that this number has now increased to 20, and the first tankers have set sail, "sailing straight through the middle of the strait." He also claimed that Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf had personally authorized the tankers' passage.

data.title: Brother Machi had some of his positions liquidated again, resulting in a total loss of $31.3 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as the market declined, Huang Licheng, known as "Brother Machi," had some of his positions liquidated again. Data shows that he continued to increase his position size after the liquidation, and his total accumulated losses have now reached $31.3 million.
